mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in nonfarm payrolls signals weakening labor demand, reducing corporate earnings visibility and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances with PAYEMS declines, or the correlation between PAYEMS changes and SPX returns falls below -0.2 in 12-month rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in housing starts combined with declining consumer price inflation creates a 'soft landing' scenario where equity valuations stabilize, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises > 2% in 3mo AND CPIAUCSL declines > 0.5% in 3mo
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in > 40% of triggered instances, or the interaction term between HOUST and CPIAUCSL shows no statistical significance (p > 0.1) in regression models.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly contraction in industrial production signals reduced corporate capacity utilization, leading to higher profit margins and positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in > 50% of INDPRO contraction periods, or the coefficient on INDPRO in a 20d return regression is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in real sales of new homes combined with declining unemployment claims indicates strong consumer demand, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises > 1.5% in 1mo AND UNRATE declines > 0.2pp in 1mo
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in > 30% of triggered instances, or the interaction term between HOUST and UNRATE shows no significance (p > 0.1) in regression models.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the federal funds rate above 2% signals tightening monetary policy, creating headwinds for equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are positive in > 50% of instances with FEDFUNDS > 2%, or the coefficient on FEDFUNDS in a 20d return regression is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in unemployment rate combined with declining payroll growth indicates labor market deterioration, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 1mo AND PAYEMS growth declines > 10k in 1mo
failure_condition: SPX returns are positive in > 40% of triggered instances, or the interaction term between UNRATE and PAYEMS shows no significance (p > 0.1) in regression models.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in GDP growth combined with rising housing starts signals economic weakness offset by strong demand, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 declines > 0.5% in 3mo AND HOUST rises > 1% in 1mo
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in > 30% of triggered instances, or the interaction term between GDPC1 and HOUST shows no significance (p > 0.1) in regression models.
